Direct, cell-level verification of visible formulas in flow_gating_1.xlsx was not possible via the artifact preview.

limitations: The .xlsx artifact could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable), so spreadsheet-internal formula visibility was validated by reading the expert_memo.md which documents formulas and results. rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Open and verify the spreadsheet to ensure all intermediate calculations and formulas are visible in-cell (no hidden formulas). In the workbook, include explicit cell references for the sensitivity-tab formulas and a final results tab with direct links to the memo's cited formulas so an auditor can reproduce every numeric transformation without relying solely on the memo text.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: The expert memo includes an explicit executive recommendation (No-go), row-level citations to embedded packet records (R1-R3), calculation summaries with formula references (e.g., MAX/MIN, MIN viability), a sensitivity table enumerating all remediation combinations with costs and budget flags, explicit flags for viability_pct < 80 and gate_range harmonization (>5 points), and a recommendation table mapping remediation options to rationale. The memo also documents missing/ambiguous inputs and lists reviewer questions and assumptions. The deterministic precheck confirms both required artifacts are present. The only remaining verification (visible formulas inside the .xlsx) could not be parsed by the preview tool, but the memo contains explicit formula references and numeric results consistent with the embedded packet, so the deliverable meets rubric success criteria.

limitations: Spreadsheet binary preview was not renderable in this environment due to openpyxl unavailability; validation relied on OOXML/XML package inspection and builder execution trace rather than cell-level rendering.

Semantic checks are based on the memo text and package-level formula presence confirmation; a human verifier with a spreadsheet viewer should open flow_gating_1.xlsx to fully validate formula visibility and traceability in a GUI.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Provide an accessible rendered spreadsheet preview (or confirm workbook opens normally in a common spreadsheet client) so reviewers can inspect the visible cell formulas directly; in the memo, add one-line clarification of how the gate_range harmonization threshold was computed (i.e., whether gate_range_pct is an absolute range per record or a pairwise difference metric) to remove any possible ambiguity for verifiers.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present. The specialist memo contains an explicit executive recommendation (No-go), row-level citations for R1-R3, raw-record values matching the embedded packet, remediation option definitions (effects and costs) with citations, a budget check against max_budget=117,600, enumerated sensitivity checks for each feasible remediation, representative post-remediation numeric examples showing multiplicative percent application, explicit needs_review flags for fractional analyst counts, assumptions, missing-input questions, and a final recommendation table. The trace shows the workbook was generated with visible formulas and cached results at the OOXML level and validated for formula presence. All checklist items in the rubric (spreadsheet raw inputs, visible formulas, sensitivity checks and budget flags, memo elements, gate_range and viability flags, needs_review marking for fractional analyst_count) are addressed.

metadata_json.verifier_issues_summary: {'id': 'I001', 'severity': 'medium', 'short': 'Spreadsheet binary not renderable in this environment', 'description': "The artifact_content_previews show the XLSX file parse_status as openpyxl_unavailable with preview_error_message 'No module named \\u0027openpyxl\\u0027'. The trace documents manual OOXML package generation and package-level inspection (workbook XML, cached values, formulas). Because no spreadsheet renderer was available, the grader could not perform a cell-level visual verification; instead validation relied on XML-level checks and builder execution trace. This limits the ability to confirm visible formula placement and exact in-GUI behavior.", 'evidence_refs': ['artifact_content_previews[ref=attempts/revised/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]]', 'trace.raw_trace_list (steps: inspect_zip_xml, run_builder)'], 'recommendation': 'Have a human verifier or CI step open flow_gating_1.xlsx in a standard spreadsheet client (or provide a rendered spreadsheet preview) to confirm visible formulas and cell-level values match the memo.', 'actionable': True}; {'id': 'I002', 'severity': 'low', 'short': 'Semantic-scoring provenance inconsistency', 'description': "Deterministic precheck metadata and explicit notes state 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated', yet the model grader outputs include semantic_score/semantic_correctness_score = 1.0 in multiple places (raw_model_output, raw_llm_output). This is an internal consistency issue: either semantic evaluation was performed by the model (contradicting the deterministic precheck note) or the semantic score was asserted without full independent verification. The grader does include limitations acknowledging reliance on memo text and package-level checks, but the deterministic precheck and model-scored semantic result are not reconciled.", 'evidence_refs': ["deterministic_precheck.metadata_json (limitations: 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated')", 'raw_model_output (semantic_score = 1.0)', 'artifact_content_previews[ref=attempts/revised/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]]'], 'recommendation': 'Clarify whether semantic correctness was intentionally judged by the model and document the exact basis (memo-only vs. full workbook cell inspection). If semantic scoring is retained, add a short justification mapping memo statements and XML-confirmed cached values to the specific rubric criteria used for the semantic score.', 'actionable': True}
